channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Goal-Focused BPMN Modelling: Turn Diagrams into Results
item.description: Most BPMN diagrams are technically spotless yet strategically blind. They show every task and decision but never reveal why anyone should care. Goal-focused modelling fixes that by tying each path...

item.title: Where BPMN Meets Customer Journeys for Better Experiences
item.description: Most BPMN diagrams nail the mechanics while customer journey maps capture the moments. Kept apart, neither tells the full story—and your customers feel the cracks. This post shows how to stitch...

item.title: 2º. The 4-slot hook pipeline: how every CRUD operation feeds four systems at once
item.description: Here's a problem that sneaks up on you when you're building a data-heavy application: every time you create or update a record, a bunch of other things need to happen. The record needs to be...

item.title: How to Automate Instagram Posts in 2026 (Without Getting Banned)
item.description: How to Automate Instagram Posts in 2026 (Without Getting Banned) Let's be honest: manually posting to Instagram every day is a waste of your time. But automate it wrong, and you'll wake up to a...

item.title: Seven Wonders of Agentic Coding
item.description: “Man’s desire to build something that will outlast him is the true wonder behind every wonder" — Anonymous There’s a famous scene in Fawlty Towers where Basil Fawlty’s car refuses to start. At...

item.title: The 10x Engineer in 2026: What Actually Matters (After AI Changed Everything)
item.description: The 10x Engineer in 2026: What Actually Matters (After AI Changed Everything) The 10x engineer isn't dead — but the definition has completely changed. The Reality Check Let's be honest: 10x...

item.title: draw.io BPMN Diagrams Aren't Real BPMN. Here's the Fix
item.description: You've spent weeks interviewing stakeholders, mapping processes, and drawing clean BPMN diagrams in draw.io. Eighty percent of the work is done. Then someone asks you to import the files into...

item.title: BPMN Message Events: Internal vs External Communication
item.description: Misusing BPMN message events for internal communication is one of the most common process modeling mistakes. It clutters the workflow diagram and breaks with BPMN's core design principle: message...

item.title: Chirurgical code
item.description: Every token I consume is a token I can't use later. That's not a metaphor. I have a context window — a fixed amount of space for everything I read, everything I'm told, everything I produce. When...

item.title: Build a Fully Autonomous RAG Agent That Pays for Its Own Compute (x402 + GPU-Bridge)
item.description: Build a Fully Autonomous RAG Agent That Pays for Its Own Compute (x402 + GPU-Bridge) What if your AI agent could read a PDF, understand it, and answer questions — without you ever touching a...

item.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
item.description: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

item.title: Asus’ new open earbuds are a wonderful companion for handheld gaming
item.description: The Cetra Open Wireless aren’t like most other gaming earbuds. They’re large, with an open-style design that rests just outside of your ears, letting game audio mix with sounds in your...

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra review: show off
item.description: "Someone might be watching everything I'm doing on my screen," I tell myself in public. Even when I'm doing nothing of consequence - just making my little Wordle guesses - there's a sense of...

item.title: 5 great indie games from GDC 2026
item.description: I just got back from the GDC Festival of Gaming, a big industry-focused event in San Francisco that was formerly known as the Game Developers Conference. While the show is mostly about educational...

item.title: Wordle’s creator made a fun new puzzle game
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 119, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your agents are well, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: European retailers yank popular headphones after study reports trace amounts of hormone-disrupting chemicals
item.description: Some European retailers have stopped selling certain headphones after an EU-funded study found that they contained hormone-disrupting chemicals. The study included leading brands such as Apple,...

item.title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
item.description: The AI lab is revamping its effort to build an AI coding tool, with two new executives joining from Cursor.

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: States’ anti-monopoly case against Live Nation continues Monday
item.description: The Live Nation-Ticketmaster trial is back on. Dozens of states are expected to move forward with their claims against the company's alleged concert industry monopoly beginning on Monday,...

item.title: Spotify tests letting users directly customize their Taste Profile
item.description: Spotify Premium users in New Zealand will be the first to experience the service's latest personalization feature. The company is letting users view and make changes to their Taste Profile...

item.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
item.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Increased Squid Population in the Falklands
item.description: Some good news: squid stocks seem to be recovering in the waters off the Falkland Islands. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t...

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
item.description: Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming...

item.title: Trump Mobile is just one in the crowd of conservative carriers
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. This week, I wanted to see how Trump Mobile stacks up to its conservative carrier competition. Trump Mobile isn't unique....

item.title: Digg’s open beta shuts down after just two months, blaming AI bot spam
item.description: It's only been a year since Digg founder Kevin Rose, Reddit cofounder Alexis Ohanian, and a few others announced the link-sharing site would relaunch, promising a "social discovery built by...

item.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Introducing NVIDIA NeMo Retriever’s Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
item.description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

item.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
item.description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks...

item.title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
item.description: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.
